ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for martabak manis (Indonesian sweet stuffed pancake). FOODON_03315091 (pancake food product) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Pancakes) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for martabak manis with chocolate and cheese filling.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for martabak manis or Indonesian sweet stuffed pancake. SNOMED CT does not have a specific concept for this food.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is the full 10-piece portion (approximately 500g), representing a 24cm diameter martabak manis shared between 2–4 people.

nutrient_source.title: Martabak Manis (Sweet Stuffed Pancake) — estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Pancakes, plain, prepared from recipe (NDB 18259) and component adjustments for chocolate, cheese, and butter filling
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central SR Legacy, supplemented by USDA Branded Food data for chocolate spread and cheddar cheese components
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for martabak manis exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy plain pancake base (NDB 18259) with proportional adjustments for the high-fat, high-sugar filling components: chocolate spread (Biscoff and chocolate), cheddar cheese, and butter. The resulting per-100g profile reflects a rich, high-calorie sweet pancake with approximately 60% carbohydrate, 20% fat, and 7% protein by energy contribution. Values are consistent with published analyses of similar Indonesian martabak manis products in food composition literature.

gi_source.title: Pancakes, made from packet mix — GI value 67 (glucose reference); analogous estimate applied to martabak manis sweet pancake base
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for martabak manis. The University of Sydney GI Database reports a GI of 67 (medium) for plain pancakes made from packet mix (glucose scale). Martabak manis shares a similar wheat-flour-and-egg batter base but contains substantially more sugar and fat from the chocolate, Biscoff, and cheese filling, which may moderately lower the effective GI through fat-mediated gastric slowing. A GI of 67 is used as a conservative analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (67 × 220g carbohydrate in 500g serving) / 100 = 147.4, rounded to 148.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and high caloric density
hypertension.guidance: Martabak manis contains approximately 280mg sodium per 100g (1,400mg per full 500g serving), which represents a significant proportion of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000mg; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 1–2 pieces and avoid adding salty toppings.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate and sugar content with medium glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: With approximately 44g carbohydrate and 22g sugar per 100g, and a medium GI of 67, a full 500g serving delivers an extremely high glycemic load of approximately 148; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this food or restrict consumption to a single piece (approximately 50g) on rare occasions, paired with a protein or fibre source to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat, trans fat, sugar, and caloric density
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Martabak manis is high in saturated fat (approximately 7.8g per 100g) and contains trace trans fat from butter and processed spreads; the combination of high saturated fat, high sugar, and high caloric density (320 kcal per 100g) makes this food a high cardiovascular risk item, and individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat it as an occasional indulgence limited to 1–2 pieces per occasion.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Martabak manis (Indonesian sweet stuffed pancake) is one of Indonesia's most iconic street foods and desserts, widely sold by street vendors and specialty restaurants across Indonesia and in Indonesian diaspora communities in Singapore and Malaysia. The dish consists of a thick, fluffy, slightly crispy-edged pancake made from a yeasted or baking-powder-leavened wheat flour batter cooked in a heavy cast-iron pan with generous amounts of butter. Once cooked, the pancake is split open and filled with a variety of sweet toppings. This particular variant — Original Choco Cheese — features melted chocolate spread, Biscoff (caramelised biscuit spread), and premium cheddar cheese, with optional peanut sprinkles for added crunch and texture contrast. The result is a rich, indulgent dessert with a soft, spongy interior, a buttery golden crust, and a complex sweet-savoury flavour profile from the combination of chocolate, caramelised biscuit, and sharp cheddar. The full serving is a 24cm diameter pancake cut into 10 pieces and weighing approximately 500g. Nutritionally, martabak manis is a high-calorie, high-carbohydrate, high-fat food (approximately 320 kcal per 100g) with significant saturated fat from butter and cheese and high sugar from the chocolate and Biscoff fillings. It carries a medium glycemic index (approximately 67) but an extremely high glycemic load per full serving, making it clinically significant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or obesity. It is best consumed as an occasional treat in small portions. The dish is vegetarian and typically halal-certified in Indonesian and Singaporean contexts.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for martabak manis with chocolate and cheese filling exists in any approved primary regional source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy plain pancake base with component-level adjustments for the high-fat, high-sugar filling. The GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using plain packet-mix pancakes as the reference food. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (plain pancake base, NDB 18259) with proportional adjustments for chocolate spread, Biscoff spread, cheddar cheese, and butter filling components. No direct approved-source entry for martabak manis coklat keju exists. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data and are not directly measured for this specific commercial product. 3. The food_group has been reclassified from the input value of 'Dairy and eggs' to 'Desserts' as martabak manis is primarily a sweet pancake dessert; dairy (cheddar cheese) is a filling component rather than the defining food group. 4. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI 67 × carbohydrate per 500g serving 220g) / 100 = 147.4, rounded to 148.0. 5. The serving size of 500g (10 pieces, 24cm diameter) is taken directly from the restaurant description and is treated as a reliable structural fact. 6. Halal status is assessed as likely true based on the Indonesian culinary context and the restaurant name (Smile Martabak), but is not formally certified in this record. 7. No -1 sentinel values are used for core macros; all secondary nutrient -1 values have been replaced with estimated numeric values from USDA component data, so no -1 sentinel documentation is required. 8. FoodOn URI FOODON_03315091 is used as closest_parent (pancake food product); no exact FoodOn term exists for martabak manis. FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Pancakes) is used as closest_parent.
